PEASANT.Definition: a poor farmer in the past

SCALES.Definition: small hard plates that cover the skin of reptiles

ARMOUR.Definition: strong metal clothing used for protection in battle

CAVES.Definition: large natural holes in rock or mountains

KNIGHTS.Definition: soldiers in the Middle Ages who fought on horseback

SLAYING.Definition: the act of killing a monster

COURAGE.Definition: bravery; the ability to face danger

SLAYER.Definition: a person who killed a dangerous creature

PATRON SAINT.Definition: a saint believed to protect the place or people

SCHOLAR.Definition: a person who studied and had a lot of knowledge

BENEVOLENT.Definition: kind and wanting to help others

FUNDAMENTALLY.Definition: in a basic or essential way

REMARKABLE.Definition: unusual or surprising in a good way

MYTHOLOGY.Definition: traditional stories about gods, heroes, and monsters

DEPICTED.Definition: shown or described in pictures or stories

ANTLER.Definition: a hard horn that grows on a deer’s head

ROBES.Definition: long loose clothes worn by important people

EMBROIDERED.Definition: decorated with patterns sewn with thread

NEUTRAL.Definition: not taking sides; neither good nor bad

UNIFORMLY.Definition: in the same way in all cases

HOARDED.Definition: collected and kept a large amount of it

JEALOUSLY.Definition: in a way that protected it strongly and did not want others to have it

VIRGIN.Definition: a young woman who had never had sex

TERRORISED.Definition: made very frightened for a long time

REPTILIAN.Definition: like a reptile, such as a snake or lizard

MORTALLY.Definition: in a way that caused death

CURSED.Definition: affected by a magic force that brought bad luck

PRIMORDIAL.Definition: very ancient; from the earliest time

HAZARD A GUESS.Definition: guess even if you are not sure

FOSSILS.Definition: remains of ancient plants or animals found in rock

INTUITIVE.Definition: easy to understand without thinking hard

WELL.Definition: a deep hole in the ground used to get water

SNOUT.Definition: the long nose of an animal

INTERPRETING.Definition: deciding the meaning of or understanding them

MYTHOLOGICAL.Definition: relating to myths or ancient stories

QUARRIES.Definition: places where stone was dug from the ground

MINES.Definition: places where minerals or metals were dug from the earth

FOSSILISED.Definition: turned into stone over a very long time

FOSSIL.Definition: the preserved remains of an ancient plant or animal

REPTILES.Definition: animals like snakes, lizards, and crocodiles

PREDATORS.Definition: animals that hunted and ate other animals

EVOLUTIONARY.Definition: related to how living things slowly change over time

VENOMOUS.Definition: able to inject poison by biting

INSTINCTIVE.Definition: natural, happening without learning or thinking

PREY.Definition: an animal that is hunted by another animal

FANGS.Definition: long sharp teeth used for biting

ROAR.Definition: a loud deep cry made by a large animal

TALONS.Definition: the sharp claws of birds of prey

BEAKS.Definition: the hard pointed mouths of birds

BIRD OF PREY.Definition: a bird that hunts other animals

PREDATOR.Definition: an animal that hunts and eats other animals

PER SE.Definition: by itself; exactly in that way

MONITOR LIZARDS.Definition: large powerful lizards that live in warm regions

INSTINCTIVELY.Definition: in a natural way, without thinking

ARCHETYPE.Definition: a common symbol or model that appears in many stories

VIRGINS.Definition: women who have never had sex

PAGANISM.Definition: old religions that existed before Christianity

MAPPED.Definition: showed or drew on a map

TAXONOMY.Definition: a scientific system for classifying living things

PALEONTOLOGY.Definition: the science that studies fossils and ancient life

MAMMOTHS.Definition: large extinct animals similar to elephants

SLOTHS.Definition: slow-moving animals that lived in trees

RELEGATED.Definition: moved to a less important place

PROPELLED.Definition: pushed forward or caused to move ahead

PROMINENTLY.Definition: in an important or noticeable way

RESONATES.Definition: creates a strong feeling of connection
